response.justification: Bogdan has a genuine track record in distributed systems, strong testing culture, and broad full-stack experience, but he fails the most critical minimum qualification by a wide margin: the role requires 8 years of C++ programming and his entire C++ exposure amounts to roughly 6 months at National Instruments plus a bachelor thesis project. His primary languages are TypeScript, JavaScript, and DLang. He also holds no formal technical leadership or cloud-platform role; his leadership activity is community-oriented (workshops, dojos, open-source) rather than organizational. Cloud architecture experience is limited to AWS CDK usage at a mid-size startup and self-managed Kubernetes for an open-source side project, well below the production-scale cloud platform engineering the role demands.
strengths: Deep, demonstrated TDD and software craftsmanship practices applied consistently across multiple professional roles
Hands-on distributed systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby engineering low-latency services handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Broad polyglot background spanning web, mobile, embedded, and DevOps tooling, showing adaptability across problem domains
concerns: C++ experience is effectively absent — approximately 6 months total versus the 8-year hard minimum qualification
No formal technical lead, staff-lead, or engineering-manager title in a cloud or infrastructure context; mentoring activity is informal and community-facing
Cloud platform depth is thin — AWS CDK and Kubernetes appear in product-engineering and side-project contexts, not large-scale internal platform ownership
